---
title: "Dataset Expansion Labs"
---

This section collects the secondary worked examples that sit alongside the main `lalonde` report.

For the one-hour overview that launches these exercises, start with the [Matching and Weighting Workshop Deck](../slides/matching_workshop.qmd) and the [Workshop Speaking Notes](../slides/matching_workshop_notes.qmd).

Use these pages as extensions of the same design-first workflow:

- start with the benchmarked policy-style examples if you want the closest match to the main report
- use the Titanic page when you want a compact teaching example for support, exact matching, and subclassification
- treat each lab as a self-contained notebook with its own assumptions, diagnostics, and tradeoffs

## Roadmap-Aligned Labs

| Lab | Dataset(s) | Current status | Link |
|---|---|---|---|
| Black Politicians Lab | `causaldata::black_politicians` | Training aid | [Open](black_politicians_lab.qmd) · [Full code](black_politicians_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](black_politicians_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](black_politicians_lab_qa_log.qmd) |
| HISP IE in Practice Lab | `evaluation.dta` converted to `evaluation.csv` from the HISP case-study bundle | Stata-to-R replication lab | [Open](hisp_ie_practice_lab.qmd) · [Full code](hisp_ie_practice_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](hisp_ie_practice_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](hisp_ie_practice_lab_qa_log.qmd) |
| NSW + CPS Benchmark Lab | `causaldata::nsw_mixtape`, `causaldata::cps_mixtape` | Benchmark lab | [Open](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab.qmd) · [Full code](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_full_code.qmd) · [Evaluated code](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_evaluated_code.qmd) · [QA log](nsw_cps_benchmark_lab_qa_log.qmd) |
| Titanic Teaching Lab | `causaldata::titanic` | Teaching lab | [Open](titanic_teaching_lab.qmd) · [Full code](titanic_teaching_lab_full_code.qmd) |

## Recommended Sequence

1. Use the workshop deck and speaking notes first if you want the `60` minute delivery version of the main report.
2. Move to the Black Politicians lab for the first post-talk matching workflow with richer covariates.
3. Use the NSW + CPS benchmark lab when you want to compare observational designs against an experimental reference.
4. Use the HISP IE in Practice lab when you want a Stata-to-R replication that walks through weaker and stronger impact-evaluation designs in the order used in the book case study.
5. Use the Titanic lab for the shortest support-focused follow-up on exact cells and subclassification.

## Working Rule

Keep the same standard across all labs: define the estimand first, inspect imbalance before adjustment, and compare balance against retention after design changes.
